title: No menu-indicator arrow for a QToolButton in InstantPopup mode - again
author: rfehren
body:
Issue #1142 was closed with reason 'That's by design. The arrow is redundant in this case.' but I don't think that the arrow is really redundant in this case. We face a real problem that we have many  QToolButton elements with a menu attached and now with Kvantum, they are indistinguishable from normal buttons. The user has no idea that a button will bring up a menu or whether it is just there for showing a status or ...

As other styles like QTCurve show the arrow, I think this is a serious drawback of Kvantum, even though we like it a lot otherwise.

Anyway, many thanks for developing such a great tool.

title: No menu-indicator arrow for a QToolButton in InstantPopup mode
author: Akrem-Alamine

title: Submenus arrow indicators don't use the pressed state
author: franksouza183
body: Submenu arrow indicators don't the SVG defined in the `pressed` state. In my tests, the one from the `focused` state is used instead. Since this does not match what the documentation states—"... whose states can be normal, **pressed**, focused, and disabled"—I believe this is a bug. Hover/focused: Pressed (it's exactly the same SVG as the hover/focused state): In this example, the expected SVG (pressed) has the exact same dark color as the text. `respect_darkness` don't seems to have any effect on this.

title: Interior background of combo box menus disappears
author: franksouza183
body: Consider the following key settings: ``` [%General] combo_menu=true ... [GeneralColors] window.color=#575757 ... [Menu] interior.element=menu ... [MenuItem] text.normal.color=#353535 ... ``` Depending on the color used in `text.normal.color` of the `MenuItem` element, the `menu-normal` part of the `Menu` element disappears in combo box menus (I’m not sure if that’s the correct name of the widget; in Kvantum Preview, you can test it on the widget located above—the one labeled "Kvantum"—and on the two widgets in the "Combos/Spins/Inputs" tab). From what I could tell during my debugging attempts, the bug occurs when the `window.color` and `text.normal.color` values are too close to each other. The problem does not occur in other menus. With `text.normal.color=#353535`: With `text.normal.color=#ffffff`:

title: dolphin and ddcui crash on Hyprland with qt6-base 6.11.1 + composite=true
author: Motaphe
body:
Hit this on Arch with `qt6-base 6.11.1-1` and `kvantum 1.1.7-1.1`. Two apps die a few seconds after launch with:

```
The Wayland connection experienced a fatal error: Invalid argument
```

No coredump, Qt's wayland client just exits 255.

### Environment

- Hyprland 0.55-git (wlroots)
- qt6-base 6.11.1-1, qt6-declarative 6.11.1-1.1
- kvantum 1.1.7-1.1
- Active theme: Catppuccin Mocha Lavender (reproduces with the bundled KvDark too, so it's not theme-specific)

### Bisected via the kvconfig

| config | dolphin | ddcui |
| --- | --- | --- |
| default kvconfig | 3/3 crash | 3/3 crash |
| `QT_STYLE_OVERRIDE=Fusion` | 3/3 clean | 3/3 clean |
| `blur_translucent=false` only | crash | crash |
| `transparent_ktitle_label=false` only | crash | crash |
| all 7 compositing sub-flags off, `composite=true` | crash | crash |
| `composite=false` | clean | clean |

So whatever Kvantum installs when `composite=true` is the trigger, not any individual Hacks checkbox.

### The weird part

Out of 14 Qt6 widget apps I have installed, only these two crash. Tested clean on Wayland with default Kvantum: ark, kvantummanager, kvantumpreview, kdeconnect-app, hyprland-share-picker, cachyos-kernel-manager, pinentry-qt, qt6ct, nextcloud, scx-manager, btrfs-assistant, kcmshell6.

ark uses the same KIO + Frameworks 6.26 stack as dolphin and is fine, so it's not a general KDE/KIO thing. Something specific to dolphin and ddcui completes the trigger on top of Kvantum's compositing path.

### Timeline

Pacman log shows the trigger was qt6-base going 6.11.0 -> 6.11.1 on May 16. Kvantum 1.1.7 was installed May 9 and worked fine for a week before the qt6 bump.

Possibly related: #1124 (NULL ext-bg-effect destroy on Niri+KDE) sounds like the same neighborhood (wlroots compositor, KDE app, wayland protocol path) but the signature is different.

No useful gdb backtrace without an instrumented rebuild since there's no SIGABRT. Happy to test patches against master if you have a guess.

Workaround for now: per-app theme variant with `composite=false`, mapped via `[Applications]` in kvantum.kvconfig.

Thanks for the recent Qt 6.11 wayland workaround sprint by the way, that was a serious chunk of work.

title: Kvantum significantly slows down application startup
author: JeanLuX
body:
Hello,

I have been using **Kvantum** for some time, and I noticed that it **significantly slows down application startup**. Apps feel **very sluggish to launch**, roughly **3 to 5 times slower**, even on fairly powerful systems (**ASUS G14 - GA402RJ** and **Lenovo ThinkPad P15s Gen2 - 20W600GNFR**).

The issue seems to get worse as the **number of displays** and their **resolutions increase**. My setup usually includes **two or three monitors**, with a mix of **1200p**, **1080p**, and **2440p** displays.

When I **disable Kvantum** and switch back to **Breeze**, applications open **almost instantly**. It honestly feels like using **a completely different computer.**

I am using **Fedora 43** and **Kvantum 1.1.6**, installed from the **official Fedora repositories**, together with the [Edna theme](https://gitlab.com/jomada/edna): 

Has anyone else experienced this issue?

title: Different Stylings/Sizes Between PanelButtonTool and ToolbarButton
author: doncsugar
body:
Hi Pedram,

I just wanted to understand if there was a way for elements to have different sizings depending on if they were in the toolbar or not. According to the Theme-Config.pdf documentation, ToolbarButton will inherit from PanelButtonTool. `text.margins` and `frame.expanded` for example do not seem to be able to be changed if it is set in PanelButtonTool. Is it possible to set these differently and if not, what is the technical limitation?

I have attached the config sections in question in case there is anything obviously wrong. The toolbar buttons are meant to be enormous and round, while the tool buttons should stay slim.

```
[PanelButtonTool]
interior.element=toolbutton
frame.element=toolbutton

frame.top=1
frame.bottom=1
frame.left=1
frame.right=1

frame.expanded.top=6
frame.expanded.bottom=6
frame.expanded.left=6
frame.expanded.right=6
frame.expansion=12

text.normal.color=#252525
text.focus.color=#252525
text.press.color=#252525
text.toggle.color=#FFFFFF
text.normal.inactive.color=#252525
text.toggle.inactive.color=#252525

; 7 is base
text.margin.top=7
text.margin.bottom=7
text.margin.left=7
text.margin.right=7
text.iconspacing=4

indicator.element=realtoolarrow
indicator.size=9


[ToolbarButton]

frame.element=tbutton
interior.element=tbutton
frame.expanded.top=19
frame.expanded.bottom=19
frame.expanded.left=19
frame.expanded.right=19
frame.expansion=38
```

title: Submenus open half an item too low on Wayland with Qt 6.11.0
author: SeriousConcept1134
body: When using Kvantum as the application style under a Wayland session, cascading submenus open with their top edge aligned to the **vertical center** of the parent menu item rather than its top edge. This makes all submenus appear shifted roughly half a menu-item height too low. This is reproducible in any Qt application with nested context menus (confirmed in Dolphin and on the KDE Plasma desktop). The issue is **not present** under XCB, and **not present** when switching the application style to Breeze under the same Wayland session, which isolates the cause to Kvantum's QStyle metric reporting. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Set application style to Kvantum (any theme — confirmed with Layan) 2. Run a Wayland session (KDE Plasma on Wayland) 3. Right-click in Dolphin or on the desktop to open a context menu 4. Hover over any item that has a submenu arrow 5. Observe the submenu popup position **Expected behaviour** The top edge of the submenu aligns with the top edge of the parent menu item that spawned it. **Actual behaviour** The top edge of the submenu aligns with the vertical center of the parent menu item — it sits approximately half a menu-item height too low. **Bisection** - **Kvantum (Wayland):** broken ❌ - **Kvantum (XCB, `QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb`):** correct ✅ - **Breeze (Wayland):** correct ✅ This points to Kvantum reporting a `PM_MenuVMargin` (or related) pixel metric value that Qt 6.11.0's Wayland backend now uses differently when constructing the `xdg_positioner` anchor rect for cascade submenus. Note that Kvantum has had to compensate for Qt submenu positioning changes before (see ChangeLog: "Compensate for a change in the vertical positioning of submenus with Qt 5.11") — this appears to be the same category of issue recurring with Qt 6.11.0. **Environment** - **Kvantum:** 1.1.6-1.1 - **qt6-base:** 6.11.0-2.1 - **qt6-wayland:** 6.11.0-1.1 - **Desktop:** KDE Plasma 6 (Wayland session) - **Distribution:** CachyOS (Arch-based) - **Kvantum theme tested:** Layan - **`[MenuItem]` section of Layan.kvconfig** (frame margins are already symmetric, ruling out a theme-level imbalance as the cause): ```ini [MenuItem] inherits=PanelButtonCommand frame=true frame.element=menuitem interior.element=menuitem indicator.element=menuitem text.normal.color=#dfdfdf text.focus.color=white text.shadow=false text.margin.top=0 text.margin.bottom=0 text.margin.left=6 text.margin.right=6 frame.top=4 frame.bottom=4 frame.left=4 frame.right=4 frame.expansion=0 ``` **Additional context** Switching between different Kvantum themes makes no difference to the alignment — the bug is present regardless of which Kvantum theme is active, confirming it is in Kvantum's C++ QStyle implementation rather than in any theme config file.

title: Alternate styles for widgets
author: albert-tomanek
body: In CSS-based theme engines (eg. Gtk CSS), you can define an alternate style for a widget and enable it by adding a class to the specific widget instance. Does Kvantum+Qt allow for anything similar? [AeroThemePlasma](https://gitgud.io/aeroshell/atp/aerothemeplasma) is working on a Windows Aero-style theme and the toolbars in Aero have several coloured variants: In CSS it would be super easy to add the second style and trigger it using a `.turquoise` style class.

title: Gentoo i3 - Floating and Resize Not Working
author: 0pLuS0
body:
Hi

System Specs;

Gentoo Openrc with i3, and Kvantum 1.1.5.

I am not sure what is going on, I've recently used Kvantum with CachyOS and Arch both with i3, and the floating resize worked just fine. I also logged in the same way through tty startx.

On all 3 distros, Gentoo, Arch, CachyOS, my setups are all the same.

These are the i3 config settings I was using in CachyOS and Arch, I assumed would work the same.

```for_window [class="Kvantum Manager"] floating enable resize set 1300 800, move position center```

I also tried using kvantummanager in the config, but still doesn't work.

I am launching Kvantum using rofi, which is loading it from the /usr/share/applications/kvantummanager.desktop which makes a desktop icon in rofi I start.

When I open Kvantum with that setting, it does float it, but the window is small, it won't resize.

xprop gives me this output

NET_WM_USER_TIME(CARDINAL) = 5612852
_NET_WM_DESKTOP(CARDINAL) = 3
_NET_WM_STATE(ATOM) = 
WM_STATE(WM_STATE):
		window state: Normal
		icon window: 0x0
I3_FLOATING_WINDOW(CARDINAL) = 1
_NET_WM_ICON_NAME(UTF8_STRING) = 
_NET_WM_ICON(CARDINAL) = 	Icon (64 x 64):

Icon (64 x 64):

XdndAware(ATOM) = BITMAP
WM_NAME(STRING) = "Kvantum Manager"
_NET_WM_NAME(UTF8_STRING) = "Kvantum Manager"
_MOTIF_WM_HINTS(_MOTIF_WM_HINTS) = 0x3, 0x3e, 0x7e, 0x0, 0x0
_NET_WM_WINDOW_TYPE(ATOM) = _NET_WM_WINDOW_TYPE_NORMAL
_XEMBED_INFO(_XEMBED_INFO) = 0x0, 0x1
WM_CLIENT_LEADER(WINDOW): window id # 0x3200009
WM_HINTS(WM_HINTS):
		Client accepts input or input focus: True
		window id # of group leader: 0x3200009
WM_CLIENT_MACHINE(STRING) = "gentoo"
_NET_WM_PID(CARDINAL) = 9038
_NET_WM_SYNC_REQUEST_COUNTER(CARDINAL) = 52428808
_GTK_APPLICATION_ID(UTF8_STRING) = "kvantummanager"
_KDE_NET_WM_DESKTOP_FILE(UTF8_STRING) = "kvantummanager"
WM_CLASS(STRING) = "kvantummanager", "Kvantum Manager"
WM_PROTOCOLS(ATOM): protocols  WM_DELETE_WINDOW, WM_TAKE_FOCUS, _NET_WM_PING, _NET_WM_SYNC_REQUEST
WM_NORMAL_HINTS(WM_SIZE_HINTS):
		user specified size: 600 by 438
		program specified minimum size: 402 by 438
		window gravity: Static

So even though I try to resize it in i3 to 1300 800 xprop shows this;

```user specified size: 600 by 438```

I know CachyOS and Arch are systemd based, but besides that, I don't get why if it worked on them, why it won't using the same WM and settings now in Gentoo... hmm

THANKS

title: Context menu opens in wrong position on dual screen
author: Morzilla
body: With my dual monitor configuration, the context menu opens in the wrong position relative to the mouse position after the click: If I move the application window on the other screen, it works without any problem. This is my setup (I'm using Kvantum 1.1.5):
